V-M-034.checks: Emergency relocation runbook must restore service on replacement RU/DE servers using existing deploy/install scripts unchanged.
V-M-034.commands: python3 -m xml.etree.ElementTree docs/plans/Phase-22.xml docs/modules/M-034.xml docs/recovery/DR-PHASE-22.xml docs/verification/V-M-034.xml && mygrace lint; manual live-required DR walkthrough using existing deploy/install scripts unchanged

module-checks.check-1: Runbook defines freeze, backup, fresh deployment, restore, validation, cutover, and rollback steps.
module-checks.check-2: Runbook explicitly forbids modifying deploy/ and install.sh as part of recovery planning.
module-checks.check-3: Runbook separates local dry-run checks from live VPS checks and labels blocked evidence.
module-checks.check-4: Runbook consumes M-035 manifest/checksum evidence before any cutover gate can pass.
module-checks.check-5: Go/no-go, rollback target, post-cutover monitoring, and incident handoff notes are required before Phase-22 can close.

evidence-matrix.evidence-1: XML parse and mygrace lint pass for Phase-22, M-034, and V-M-034.
evidence-matrix.evidence-2: Restore walkthrough records each operator decision point without printing secrets.
evidence-matrix.evidence-3: Live RU/DE replacement walkthrough proves backend, frontend, admin, auth, billing read path, Redis, and VPN handshake checks.
evidence-matrix.evidence-4: DNS/IP/operator cutover is not considered verified by local documentation checks.
evidence-matrix.evidence-5: VPN validation proves RU/DE tunnel health, AWG profile parity, existing client config behavior, and explicit rotation guard on the restore target.
evidence-matrix.evidence-6: Application validation proves backend health, database, Redis, user/admin auth, billing read path, device listing, and frontend/admin reachability on the restore target.
evidence-matrix.evidence-7: Cutover evidence records go/no-go decision, rollback target, post-cutover smoke checks, and monitoring window.

scenarios.scenario-1: Operator can follow the runbook from a backup bundle to a validated replacement deployment.
scenarios.scenario-2: Restore procedure loses device-client linkage or invalidates existing configs without explicit rotation.
scenarios.scenario-3: Recovery steps print secrets, tokens, passwords, private keys, or raw .env content into logs.
scenarios.scenario-4: Runbook requires editing deploy/ or install.sh instead of using the existing approved deployment flow.
scenarios.scenario-5: Cutover proceeds without backend, frontend, admin, auth, billing read, and VPN handshake evidence.
scenarios.scenario-6: Local dry-run evidence is recorded as if it proves live VPS recovery readiness.

autonomy-safety.safe-local: Agents may update runbook text, checklists, verification metadata, and dry-run evidence labels.
autonomy-safety.requires-operator: Agents must not perform live cutover, rotate client configs, expose secrets, or change deploy/install scripts without explicit operator control.
